case_title: DAYMEN US INC. VS. KENU, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

NOTICE OF DEMURRER OF DEFENDANTS KENU, INC. AND DAVID YAO TO COMPLAINT FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, TORTIOUS INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS, CONVERSION, BREACH OF THE DUTY OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, TRESPASS TO CHATTELS, UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES, SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF A CONTRACT, OWNERSHIP, MISAPPROPRIATION OF TRADE SECRETS, AND BREACH OF CONFIDENCE (TRANSACTION ID # 58016862) FILED BY DEFENDANT KENU, INC. YAO, DAVID

PARTIES SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE WHICH MAY BE E-MAILED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Continued To Feb-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Seal Portions Of Briefs And Evidence In Support Of Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration By Defendants Kenu, Inc. And David Yao
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Virginia DeMarchi of Fenwick & West LLP telephone number 650-988-8500 for Plaintiff Daymen US, Inc.
Marcus Hall of Troiutman Sanders telephone number 415-477-5716 for Defendant Kenu, Inc. and David Yao.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KENU INC and DAVID YAO'S Motion To Seal Portions Of Briefs And Evidence In Support Of Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

(Redacted) Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration By Defendants Kenu, Inc. And David Yao
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Virginia DeMarchi of Fenwick & West LLP telephone number 650-988-8500 for Plaintiff Daymen US, Inc.
Marcus Hall of Troiutman Sanders telephone number 415-477-5716 for Defendant Kenu, Inc. and David Yao.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KENU INC and DAVID YAO'S Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration is granted in part. This action is stayed pending a determination in the Minn/DayMen arbitration only of the issue of the enforceability and scope of the so-called first "carve-out" in paragraph 5 of the February 11, 2011 separation agreement between Mr. Minn and Joby, Inc., including whether that carve-out permitted Mr. Minn to engage in the work that led to the asserted inventions documented in and protected by the 707 and 714 patents. This issue is a logical and legal predicate to the determination of one or more of the claims asserted by DayMen in this action and is covered by the arbitration clause of paragraph 9 of the February 11, 2011 separation agreement between Mr. Minn and Joby. DayMen has not advanced a persuasive argument why this issue should not be resolved first or why it isn't covered by the arbitration clause agreed to by its predecessor Joby. If and when an arbitration award on this issue is confirmed, then the stay is lifted and DayMen may proceed with its claims in this case. Order for the same signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2016 Continued To Jul-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Kenu Inc And David Yao'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendants Kenu Inc And David Yao'S Demurrer To Complaint Part 2 of 2 is off calendar as a duplicate entry for purposes of entry of tentative ruling only. (302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. VIRGINIA K. DEMARCHI, ESQ. of Fenwick & West LLP, Tel No. 650-988-8500 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MARCUS HALL, ESQ. of Troutman Sanders LLP, Tel No. 415-477-5700 appearing for Defendants Kenu, Inc. and David Yao.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: As to Defendants Kenu, Inc. and David Yao's Demurrer to Complaint, Defendants' demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action for specific performance. Defendants' demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action for tortious interference with contractual relations, and the ninth cause of action for declaratory relief. Defendants' demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to every other claim: the first cause of action for breach of contract cause to incorporate the allegations of the eighth cause of action for specific performance; the third cause of action for conversion; the fifth cause of action for trespass to chattels; the sixth and seventh causes of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law; the eleventh cause of action for breach of confidence; the fourth cause of action breach of the implied covenant to allege a breach of an implied contractual obligation if plaintiff can do so in good faith; tenth cause of action misappropriation of trade secrets to more specifically identify the alleged trade secrets. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The court takes judicial notice of the existence of the documents in the request for judicial notice, but does not accept the truth of their contents or the meaning ascribed to them by defendants. The breach of contract claim is adequately pled, but that claim should also allege entitlement to specific performance, which is only a remedy and not a separate claim. The interference with contract claim adequately alleges a claim not preempted by CUTSA and does not require any additional allegations regarding Kenu's knowledge of the contract. The conversion, trespass, UCL and breach of confidence claims are based on misuse of information that is claimed to be secret, confidential and/or proprietary and thus those claims are preempted by CUTSA. The breach of implied covenant claim as currently pled is duplicative of the breach of contract claim and does not allege a breach of an implied obligation. The declaratory relief claim is adequately pled. The trade secrets claim does not adequately identify the alleged trade secrets. While it is permissible to identify trade secret information in relation to products and a general description such as the "formula for Diet Coke" suffices, at a minimum each product must be identified and there must be identification of what information pertaining to each product is alleged to be trade secrets. For instance, the allegation that "drawings for Joby Grip Tight product XYZ" are trade secrets is an adequate allegation. If and when that is accomplished, the allegations will likely satisfy CCP 2019.210 so that plaintiff may pursue discovery regarding its trade secret claim. Parties shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.
